value.market_read: WTI Curve: WTI front-month settled at $70.22/bbl in the prior session, supported by the constructive draw signal from the latest WPSR (week ending June 19, released June 24). Yesterday's reported Cushing draw tightened prompt balances and drove the August contract higher into settlement, reinforcing the backwardated structure. The curve is firmly backwardated, with the M1–M12 spread at $3.36 — a moderate backwardation that prices near-term supply tightness rather than a storage glut. The August/September M1–M2 spread sits at +$0.31, consistent with a market willing to pay up for prompt barrels. Watch: Whether subsequent Cushing draws sustain the prompt premium or whether OPEC+ barrels flatten the front of the curve. Brent Curve: Brent front-month settled at $73.48/bbl, holding a premium to WTI on firmer waterborne demand and continued Hormuz risk in the background. The Brent curve is more gently backwardated, with M1–M12 at $2.06 — flatter than WTI, reflecting global balances that are tighter at the prompt but less stressed across the strip. Note the modest kink mid-curve (the Oct contract at $73.47 nearly matching front), signaling some near-dated supply comfort before backwardation reasserts. The shallower Brent backwardation versus WTI underscores that the prompt tightness story is more Cushing-centric than global. Watch: Whether geopolitical risk premium builds or fades, which would steepen or flatten the front of the Brent strip. RBOB Gasoline Curve: RBOB front-month settled at $2.8821/gal, reflecting peak summer-driving pull on prompt gasoline. The curve is steeply backwardated through the summer contracts, collapsing from $2.88 front to roughly $2.19 by early 2027 before the winter-to-summer 2027 transition lifts deferred contracts back toward $2.40. The M1–M12 spread of +$0.4838 is the steepest backwardation in the complex, a textbook summer-demand signal pricing tight prompt gasoline supply. The sharp August/September drop (~16 c/gal) captures the seasonal demand cliff past peak driving season. Watch: Refinery run rates and gasoline draws through July — any stumble into peak season would further bid the prompt. ULSD Diesel Curve: ULSD front-month settled at $3.1572/gal, with the distillate complex firm on tight global middle-distillate balances. The curve is smoothly and consistently backwardated, declining steadily from $3.16 front to $2.69 a year out, with an M1–M12 spread of +$0.4698 — nearly matching gasoline but without the seasonal kink. This monotonic backwardation reflects structural distillate tightness rather than a seasonal spike, consistent with low global diesel inventories and firm export pull. The steady decline signals the market sees prompt tightness easing only gradually across the strip. Watch: Distillate inventory trajectory and any early-season heating or export demand that could re-steepen the front.
value.kpi_wti: WTI settled at $70.22/bbl in the prior session, lifted by the constructive Cushing-draw signal from the latest WPSR and holding a backwardated front.
value.kpi_brent: Brent settled at $73.48/bbl, retaining a premium to WTI on firmer waterborne demand and lingering Hormuz risk.
value.kpi_rbob: RBOB settled at $2.8821/gal, reflecting peak summer-driving demand pulling hard on prompt gasoline supply.
value.kpi_ulsd: ULSD settled at $3.1572/gal, firm on structurally tight global middle-distillate balances and steady export pull.
value.kpi_bw: The Brent–WTI spread sits at $3.26 — relatively narrow, signaling weak US export arb economics; WTI would need to fall (or Brent rise) to re-widen the spread and restore export competitiveness.
value.kpi_curve_regime: All four complexes are backwardated, with products (RBOB and ULSD M1–M12 near +$0.48) steeper than crude — prompt tightness dominates over any storage-surplus play.
